title: AI trading tools are exploding in 2025. A list of AI-powered investing & trading tools for retail investors
author: elena-tertie
selftext:
I’ve been testing a bunch of AI investing tools lately and wanted to share a quick overview of what I’ve found so far. Nothing ranked, nothing hyped — just a simple look at what each tool does well, where it falls short, and who it actually fits.

A few examples:  
• Trade Ideas is great for active setups but pricey  
• TrendSpider is a beast for charting and automation  
• WallStreetZen is clean for fundamentals but limited on technicals  
• Danelfin focuses on AI scoring, but can feel data-heavy  
• trade & tonic explains signals in plain language and mixes fundamentals + technicals

Plus platforms like TradingView, Benzinga Pro, Tickeron, Morningstar, and others.

If you’re trying to figure out which type of AI tool fits your style - long-term investing, swing trading, or quick technical setups - this gives a clear starting point without going into overload here.

Full breakdown is here if you want the detailed comparisons:  
[https://tradeandtonic.com/blog/which-ai-trading-tool-is-right-for-you-in-2025](https://tradeandtonic.com/blog/which-ai-trading-tool-is-right-for-you-in-2025)

Curious what everyone else is using right now or what’s been working for you.
